title: HELPING HAND - 211info
text: MAIN OFFICE HELPING HAND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency financial assistance to address basic needs, including emergency lodging for up to three nights, prescription assistance for non-narcotic medications (twice per 12-month period), auto repairs up to $200 (once per 12-month period), laundry vouchers up to $10 per individual and $20 per family (twice per 12-month period), and rent payment or deposit assistance up to $300, but not more than 70% of one month's rent (once per 18-month period). 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: -Laundry vouchers: Unrestricted -Prescription assistance: Must have a written or phoned-in prescription from Walmart or Safeway -Rent assistance: Must be facing immediate eviction, potential homelessness, or experiencing homelessness -Lodging: Must be transitioning to permanent housing -Auto repairs: Must be in need of transportation to employment, medical appointment, or school Hours: Tuesday-Friday 9am-11:30am by appointment only (541) 276-3418 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 276-3418 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or call for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Financial assistance programs: proof of income, proof of address, Social Security cards, photo ID, rental/lease agreement LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Cities of Adams, Athena, Helix, Meacham, Pendleton, Pilot Rock, Reith, Ukiah, Umatilla Reservation (zip code 97801), and Weston TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Enter from the front lobby doors on SE Byers Avenue, south side of the building. Services: Homeless Motel Vouchers Laundry Vouchers Prescription Expense Assistance Rent Payment Assistance Rental Deposit Assistance Repair Services Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY FOOD - HOME DELIVERED MEALS HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION HOUSING/SHELTER - HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - WEATHERIZATION SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES INFORMATION SERVICES - FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM MONEY MANAGEMENT - CAPECO MONEY MANAGEMENT UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. MAIN OFFICE
